plan_name: HTTP-over-UDS client: DaemonClient and ConnectionMode abstraction

tasks.name: Task 1: Add UDS HTTP client dependencies to server crate
tasks.notes: Added hyper 1 (client+http1), hyper-util 0.1 (tokio), http-body-util 0.1 to workspace deps and server crate. Aligns with axum 0.8 transitive hyper 1.8.1. Two new transitive crates pulled: try-lock 0.2.5, want 0.3.1.

tasks.name: Task 2: Implement DaemonClient with HTTP-over-UDS and ConnectionMode enum
tasks.notes: Created daemon_client.rs with DaemonClient (10 endpoint methods), ConnectionMode enum (Daemon/Direct), DaemonError, resolve_socket_path, detect_connection_mode. Also added Deserialize to 16 store/server types that previously only derived Serialize — required for DaemonClient to deserialize daemon JSON responses into the same types.

decisions.description: Added serde::Deserialize derive to all store query result structs and HealthResponse — these types previously only had Serialize, but DaemonClient deserialization requires both
decisions.rationale: Plan must_haves state 'same Serialize/Deserialize types used by the store layer' which implicitly requires Deserialize. Adding it is a backward-compatible change (no existing Deserialize consumers are affected).

decisions.description: export_session() returns raw Vec<u8> instead of a typed JSON deserialization, since export responses may be markdown or CSV
decisions.rationale: The export endpoint returns different Content-Types depending on the format parameter. Deserializing non-JSON formats as JSON would fail, so the method returns raw bytes for the caller to handle.

decisions.description: Used a minimal custom urlencoded() function instead of adding a percent-encoding crate
decisions.rationale: The query parameter values used by this client are simple strings (session IDs, search terms, format names). A full URL-encoding crate would be overkill. The custom function handles RFC 3986 unreserved characters correctly.

deviations.description: Added Deserialize to 16 store/server types in query.rs, fts.rs, and health.rs
deviations.impact: Backward-compatible — only adds a new derive. Required for DaemonClient to function as intended.
